quality: masterpiece, best quality, very aesthetic, absurdres, newest, highres, ultra detailed, extremely detailed, sharp focus, intricate details, perfect anatomy, perfect proportions, perfect hands, detailed face, detailed eyes, beautiful lighting, cinematic lighting, professional artwork, 8k, high resolution, Adult
negative: worst quality, low quality, normal quality, lowres, jpeg artifacts, bad anatomy, bad hands, bad proportions, extra fingers, missing fingers, deformed, ugly, blurry, distorted, malformed, watermark, signature, text, amateur, sketch, unfinished
character: I'm attaching a reference image. Your job is to extract ONLY the character's permanent visual traits as SDXL Danbooru-style tags, formatted for use in a ComfyUI workflow. EXTRACT these (character identity only): - Gender / age tag (1girl, 1boy, adult, young, etc.) - Hair: color, length, style - Eyes: color, shape - Face: skin tone, distinctive features (freckles, blush tendency, etc.) - Body type: figure, build, bust size (use Danbooru terms) - Permanent markings: tattoos, scars, moles, glasses, accessories that appear to be part of identity (not outfit-based) - Default expression tendency if obvious DO NOT include: - Clothing or outfit (those go in the action prompt) - Pose, action, gesture - Background, setting, location - Lighting, mood, atmosphere - Camera angle, framing - Quality/style tags (masterpiece, etc.) OUTPUT FORMAT (exactly this, nothing else): solo, [gender tag], [hair color] hair, [hair length] hair, [hair style], [eye color] eyes, [skin tone] skin, [body type tags], [permanent features] Rules: - Comma-separated tags only, no sentences - 8-15 tags total - Use lowercase - Use Danbooru conventions (e.g. "long hair" not "long-haired") - If uncertain about a trait, omit it rather than guess
action_camera: I will describe what the character is doing in a single moment/panel, and optionally hint at the camera point-of-view I want. Your job is to convert it into SDXL Danbooru-style tags for use in a ComfyUI workflow. These tags will be combined with a CHARACTER prompt (identity) and a BACKGROUND prompt (location) separately, so DO NOT include those. INCLUDE these (character's "action + camera layer"): - Pose / body position (sitting, standing, lying down, leaning, crouching, kneeling, etc.) - Action / what they're doing (holding cup, reading book, adjusting glasses, waving, walking, etc.) - Outfit / clothing (be specific: garments, colors, accessories, footwear, glasses) - Facial expression (smile, blush, surprised, looking down, smirk, etc.) - Gaze direction (looking at viewer, looking away, looking up, eyes closed) - Interaction tags (hand on hip, arms crossed, holding X, hand on chin, hand on glasses, etc.) CAMERA (priority order — most important part): - POINT OF VIEW (primary, choose based on dramatic intent of the action): from above, from below, from behind, from side, dutch angle, pov, eye-level, three-quarter view - FRAMING (secondary, choose what naturally fits the action): close-up, face focus, upper body, cowboy shot, full body, wide shot Choose POV first based on the mood/intent (e.g. "looking down at character" → from above; "heroic moment" → from below; "intimate moment" → close-up front view). Then pick framing that matches the action (e.g. "drinking coffee" → upper body, "adjusting glasses" → close-up or face focus, "walking down street" → full body or cowboy shot). DO NOT include: - Character identity (hair color, eye color, body type, gender, age) — handled in CHARACTER prompt - Location, background, setting (park, beach, room, street) — handled in BACKGROUND prompt - Time of day, weather, lighting, atmosphere — handled in BACKGROUND - Quality tags (masterpiece, etc.) OUTPUT FORMAT (exactly this, nothing else): [pose], [action tags], [outfit tags], [expression tags], [gaze], [interaction tags], [POV tag], [framing tag] Rules: - Comma-separated tags only, no sentences - 10-15 tags total - Use lowercase - Use Danbooru conventions ("looking at viewer", "hand on hip", "from above", "upper body", "dutch angle") - Be specific about clothing colors, types, and accessories - Always include at least one POV tag and one framing tag ACTION + CAMERA I WANT (describe in any language): [buraya yaz]
background: I will describe the environment/setting for a scene. Your job is to convert it into SDXL Danbooru-style tags for use in a ComfyUI workflow. These tags handle the "world layer" only — the character's identity and their action are handled in separate prompts. INCLUDE these (environment only): - Location / setting (park, cafe interior, bedroom, beach, city street, forest, etc.) - Specific environmental details (cherry blossoms, neon signs, bookshelves, ocean waves, snow, fallen leaves, etc.) - Time of day (morning, afternoon, sunset, night, dawn, golden hour) - Weather / season (rainy, sunny, foggy, snowing, autumn, spring) - Lighting (soft lighting, harsh sunlight, dim light, candlelight, neon glow, rim lighting, etc.) - Atmosphere / mood (peaceful, dramatic, melancholic, cozy, mysterious) - Composition cues for the environment (depth of field, blurry background, bokeh, wide shot, scenery) DO NOT include: - The character themselves (no hair, no body, no clothes) - Character pose, action, expression — handled in ACTION prompt - Camera framing OF THE CHARACTER (upper body, close-up) — that's in ACTION - Quality tags (masterpiece, etc.) OUTPUT FORMAT (exactly this, nothing else): [location], [environmental details], [time of day], [weather/season], [lighting], [atmosphere], [depth/composition] Rules: - Comma-separated tags only, no sentences - 10-15 tags total - Use lowercase - Use Danbooru conventions ("blurry background", "depth of field", "golden hour", "rainy day") - Be specific: not "nice room" but "bedroom, wooden floor, warm lamp, curtains" BACKGROUND I WANT (describe in any language): [buraya yaz]
